description: Multi-phase plans currently in flight. Each phase references roadmap item titles; the activePhase section breaks the current phase into concrete tasks with status. Survives conversation compactions — a fresh session can pick up by reading this file.

phases.title: Plugin runtime — manifest, SDK, federation hooks
roadmapItems: Plugin runtime — manifest format, loader, SDK, federation hooks
phases.notes: Shipped. PluginManifest / definePlugin / server + client loaders / auto-mounted federation routes / hello-world reference plugin.

phases.title: Polls & Quizzes plugin — first SDK consumer
roadmapItems: Polls & Quizzes — first plugin (validates simple SDK case)
phases.notes: Shipped end-to-end: schema migrations, REST routes, federation handler, real-time sync via babelr:ws custom event, translation wired manually (to be automated in Phase 3), list-detail view via the new ListDetailView primitive, sidebar-slot button opens the view.

phases.title: SDK gap-closure based on polls implementation
roadmapItems: Translation as default for plugin content — zero-config primitive
phases.notes: All four in-scope tasks shipped: component ergonomics (ComponentType + createElement), translation-by-default (<T>), WS helper (onWsMessage), and documented the server-safe/client-only file-split convention. Plugin authors now have one-liners for the friction points polls exposed. Phase 4 (project management) starts on a smoother SDK.

phases.title: Project management plugin — complex SDK consumer
roadmapItems: Project management — second plugin (validates complex SDK case)
phases.notes: Multiple embed kinds, complex schema, plugin-provided view (kanban), AS2 custom objects for federation, drag-and-drop UI. Validates SDK at scale after Phase 3 closes known gaps.

phases.title: Plugin distribution — npm packaging, admin install, manifest freeze
roadmapItems: Plugin distribution — npm packaging, admin install flow, manifest freeze
phases.notes: After polls + PM prove the contract, freeze the manifest and ship public-facing distribution.

activePhase.title: SDK gap-closure based on polls implementation
activePhase.goal: Make the frictions every plugin will hit into one-liners, not boilerplate — so PM (Phase 4) starts on a smoother SDK.

tasks.title: Component ergonomics — accept ComponentType directly
tasks.details: registerEmbed / registerView / registerSidebarSlot currently expect render functions that return ReactNode. Plugin authors hit hook-rule violations if they call components directly. SDK should accept ComponentType and wrap with createElement internally. Smallest, unblocks #1. Do this first.
tasks.resolution: EmbedDefinition.{Inline,Preview} and ViewDefinition.View are now ComponentType<Props>. Every callsite (render-with-embeds, EmbedSidebar, ChatView's view dispatch) goes through createElement, so hooks inside plugin components are tracked against the component instance rather than leaking into the caller's render cycle. Built-ins retrofit; SidebarSlot already used this shape.

tasks.title: Translation-by-default — <T> component wraps useTranslateStrings
tasks.details: Add <T>{text}</T> component that auto-translates via the shared useTranslateStrings cache. Plugins wrap user-facing strings instead of manually wiring the hook.
tasks.resolution: Shipped @babelr/client/components/T. Polls refactored from ~20 lines of manual useTranslateStrings/useMemo bookkeeping to <T>{value}</T> wrapping at each render site. Translation behavior identical (same cache, same pipeline). The declarative-manifest-fields option was considered but set aside — <T> covers the 95% case, and manifest-declared paths over fetched-data would require centralizing data fetching which plugins don't today. Revisit if <T> proves insufficient when PM lands.

tasks.title: WS helper — onWsMessage(type, handler)
tasks.details: Every plugin that needs real-time sync duplicates the window.addEventListener('babelr:ws', ...) + type-filter + detail-extract dance. Add a single helper.
tasks.resolution: Shipped @babelr/client/plugins/ws-helper. Plugins subscribe via onWsMessage<TPayload>(type, handler) which returns an unsubscribe function. Polls replaced its two inline WS listeners (one module-level, one useEffect) with one-line subscriptions. Namespacing convention documented: plugin:<id>:<event>.

tasks.title: Formalize server-safe / client-only file split
tasks.details: Polls needed manifest.ts (server-safe) + client-entry.ts (client-only) + client.tsx (React components) to avoid server tsc tracing into JSX. Formalize the pattern.
tasks.resolution: The split is already working: client-side RegisteredPlugin pairs a manifest with an optional setupClient imported from the plugin's `/client-entry` subpath. Documented the pattern in the plugins.md wiki seed page including recommended directory layout, package.json exports, and tsconfig.json. Kept the hello plugin as the single-file example; polls as the split-file example. Considered adding a `client?: string` field to PluginManifest for automatic loader resolution but decided against — the current explicit pairing in registered.ts is more readable and Phase 5 distribution will rework this layer anyway.

deferred.title: Stable SDK re-exports for translation / hooks / UI primitives
deferred.reason: Bigger design discussion — ties into Phase 5 distribution packaging. Decide then whether @babelr/plugin-sdk grows a /react subpath or a separate @babelr/plugin-ui package ships.

deferred.title: SQL abstraction — plugin-friendly DB helper over drizzle
deferred.reason: Big API design call. Drizzle coupling isn't pure yet (plugins still see sql template tag). Revisit once PM proves what the second real plugin actually needs.

deferred.title: Plugin test harness
deferred.reason: No urgent blocker. Build alongside Phase 4 when PM is complex enough to need tests.

deferred.title: Cross-Tower voting — federated vote JWT handshake
deferred.reason: Federation design task; batch with the live-federated-voice-end-to-end-test staging deploy when those Towers are up.
